subject: [PATCH v3 2/2] mm/vmpressure: move v1 userspace eventfd code into memcontrol-v1.c

reviews.author: Usama Arif (author)
reviews.summary: Author acknowledged a missing Ack from Shakeel in the patch history and apologized for the oversight.
reviews.raw_body:
Shakeel had acked the previous version, but I forgot to carry it over,
sorry about that!

2026-06-30.patch_summary: This patch moves the v1 userspace eventfd code from mm/vmpressure.c into a separate file, mm/memcontrol-v1.c, which is only built when CONFIG_MEMCG_V1 is enabled. This separation reduces the size of mm/vmpressure.c and makes it easier to see the full footprint of the deprecated cgroup v1 memory controller. The patch also introduces static-inline no-op stubs for the !MEMCG_V1 case, allowing callers to avoid ifdefs. The goal is to eventually make vmpressure CONFIG_MEMCG_V1 only, with a medium-term plan to introduce a PSI-based socket-pressure path and keep vmpressure available as an opt-out behind a defconfig.
